questions.text: How did Charles feel about space travel as a boy?
options: He thought it was unlikely to happen
He regarded it as more than science fiction
He was fascinated by the idea of it
He showed no particular interest in it
questions.answer: He was fascinated by the idea of it

questions.text: What did Charles consider to be the hardest part of the training?
options: feeling trapped in the heavy spacesuit
endlessly practising the lunar surface landing
constantly being afraid of making a mistake
being unable to move his arms and hands
questions.answer: feeling trapped in the heavy spacesuit

questions.text: What was Charles’s reaction when he first found out he was going to the moon?
options: He realised he had to be cautious
He felt proud to be given the opportunity
He tried to control his excitement
He reflected on his chances of survival.
questions.answer: He felt proud to be given the opportunity

questions.text: How did the crew feel when they had landed on the moon?
options: They felt as if they were coming home
They realised they had achieved something special
They were afraid of what they might find on the surface
They were worried about how they would take off again
questions.answer: They realised they had achieved something special

questions.text: What feature of the moon made the greatest impact on Charles?
options: the brightness of the
the vastness of the sky
the loneliness of the place
the absence of any stars

questions.text: What does Charles feel was the most memorable part of his mission?
options: nearly falling into a crater
walking on the moon’s surface
seeing things never seen before
holding a piece of the moon
